hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.en:
The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, would say in 
the Qunoot (supplication) of the Witr prayer:  
"O’ Allah {SWT}, guide me among those You have guided, and grant me well-being among 
those You have granted well-being to, and take me under Your care among those You 
have taken into Your care, and bless me in what You have given, and protect me from 
the evil of what You  have decreed. Indeed, You decree and no one can decree over 
You. Glory be to You, Lord {AZJ} of the House. I seek Your forgiveness and I turn to You in 
repentance. I believe in You and rely upon You. There is no power nor strength except 
with You, O’ Most Merciful." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 487 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 761 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 71, H. 1402

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1404  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
إَّلْق َّزَّح ِ . 
ي م هذ إ إلذعاء . وإلق ن وب  ق ى  إلوي ز كق ن وي ك  ي وم إلح معه  ، ي م ي ق ول ق ن ل ذعان ك  لن ق سك  :" إللهم ي م ن ورك  ق هذي ث  ق لك  إلحمذ 
رن ن ا ، ون سطث  ي ذك   ق ا عطي ث  ق لك  إلحمذ رن ن ا، وعط م جلمك  ق عق وب  ق لك  إلحمذ رن ن ا، وج هك  إ كزم إلوج وة وج هي ك  ج ي ز 
إلح هاب  وعطي ي ك  إ ق ص ل إلعطي اب  وإ هن و ها، ي طاع رن ن ا ق ي س كز، وي عصى رن ن ا ق ي ع ق ز لمن  س ن ث ، ي ح ي ث  إلمص طز وي كس ف  إلص ز 
وي س ق ى  إلسق ي م وي ن ح ى  من  إلكزب  إلعط ي م، لأ ي ح ز ى  ن الأ ن ك  إ جذ   ولأ ي حصى  ن عمان ك  ق ول ق ان ل، إللهم إ لي ك  رق عث  إلأن صار 
ون ق لث  إلأق ذإم، ومذب  إلأ عن اق ، ورق عث  إلأ ي ذى ، وذعي ث  ن الأ لسن  وإ لي ك  سزهم ون ح وإهم ق ى  إلأ عمال ، رن ن ا إع ق ز لن ا وإرجمن ا  
وإق ي ح ن ي ن ن ا ون ي ن  ق ومن ا ن الحق  وإ ن ث  ج ي ز إلق اي حي ن ، إللهم إ ن ا ن س كو إ لي ك  ع ي ن ه  ن ن ي ن ا عن ا ، وس ذة  إلز ما ن  علي ن ا، ووق وع إل ق ي ن  
ن ن ا، وي ط اهز إلأ عذإء علي ن ا وكي زة  عذون ا وق له  عذذن ا ق زح   ذ لك  ي ا رب  ن ق ي ح من ك  ي عح له، ون صز من ك  ي عز ة، وإ مام عذل ي ط هزة  
إ لي ه إلحق  رب  إلعالمي ن   " ي م ي ق ول: إ سي ع ق ز إلله رن ى  وإ ي وب  إ لي ه سن عي ن  مزة   وي عوذ  ن الله من  إلن ار كي ي زإ .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws}, peace be upon him and his family, said:  
"The one who prolongs their Qunoot (supplication during prayer) the longest in this 
worldly life will be granted the longest rest on the Day of Resurrection at the Standing 
(before Allah {SWT})”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 487 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 33 • 
Al-Amali (by Al -Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 509 • Al -Waezin, volume 2, page 328 • Sulwat al -Hazin, Part 1, p. 254 • 
Meftah al-Falah, Part 1, p. 309 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 757 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 291 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 
6, p. 292 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 7, p. 303 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1405  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عُّمَّزُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّز ِي ذَّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال 
رَّن ِّى  وَّ إ َّي ُّوب ُّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ سَّن ْعِي ن َّ مَّز َّة ً وَّ وَّإط َّث َّ عَّلَّى ذ َّلِك َّ جَّي  َّى ي َّمْص ِى َّ سَّن َّه   كَّي َّن َّهُّ إَّلل َّهُّ عِن ْذَّةُّ مِن إَّلْمُّسْي َّع ْق ِزِي ن َّ ن ِالْأ َّسْحٰارِ   
وَّ وَّج َّن َّث ْ لَّهُّ إَّلْح َّن  َّه ُّ وَّ إَّلْمَّع ْق ِزَّة ُّ مِن َّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"The Qunoot (supplication during prayer) on the Day of Jumu’ah consists of glorifying 
Allah {SWT}, sending blessings upon the Prophet of Allah {SWT}, and words of relief 
(Kalimatu-l-Faraj)”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
In the Qunoot of Witr prayer, say as you do on Jumu’ah, and then say before 
supplicating for yourself:  
'O’ Allah {SWT}, Your Light has completed and guided, so for You is praise, our Lord {AZJ}. 
You have extended Your Hand and granted, so for You is praise, our Lord {AZJ}. Your 
forbearance has magnified, so You forgave, for You is praise, our Lord {AZJ}.  
Your Face (The Aimmah {a.s}) is the most honored of faces, Your direction the best of 
directions, and Your gifts the finest and most delightful. You are obeyed, our Lord {AZJ}, 
and You are thankful; You are disobeyed, our Lord {AZJ}, and You forgive whom You wish.  
You respond to the distressed, relieve hardship, cure the ill, and save from severe 
trials. None can fully recompense Your blessings or enumerate Your gifts,  
O’ Allah {SWT}. To You the eyes have turned, the steps have walked, the necks have been 
raised, the hands have been lifted, and tongues have called You. And to You belongs 
their secrets and declarations in deeds.  
Our Lord {AZJ}, forgive us and have mercy upon us, and decide between us and our 
people in truth, for You are the best of deciders. O’ Allah {SWT}, we complain to You of 
the absence of our Prophet from among us, the difficulty of the time upon us, the 
occurrence of trials with us, the intensification of enemies against us, and our few 
numbers against their many.  
Grant relief, O’ Lord {AZJ}, through a hastened opening from You, a victory You make 
strong, and a just Imam to whom You show truth, O’ Lord {AZJ} of the worlds.'" 
Then, say: "I seek Allah’s {SWT} forgiveness, my Lord {AZJ}, and I repent to Him" seventy 
times and seek refuge in Allah {SWT} often from the Fire. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1406  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  ي َّعْق ُّورٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ى  إَّلْوَّي ْزِ سَّن ْعِي ن َّ مَّز َّة ً 
ي َّن ْصِث ُّ ي َّذَّك َّ إَّلْي ُّسْزَّى وَّ ي َّعُّذ ُّ  ن ِالْي ُّمْن َّى إَّلأِسْي ِع ْق َّارَّ وَّ كَّان َّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ي َّسْي َّع ْق ِزُّ إَّلل َّهَّ ق ِى  إَّلْوَّي ْزِ  
سَّن ْعِي ن َّ مَّز َّة ً وَّ ي َّق ُّول .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Umar ibn Yazid that Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever says during his Witr prayer (while performing the single -unit prayer of Witr) 
'Astaghfirullah Rabbi wa Atubu Ilayh' (I seek forgiveness from Allah {SWT}, my Lord {AZJ}, 
and I turn to Him in repentance) seventy times, and continues to do so regularly until 
a year passes, Allah {SWT} will write him among 'those who seek forgiveness in the early 
hours of the morning,' and Paradise and forgiveness from Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and 
Glorious, will become obligatory for him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 489 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 279 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 71, H. 1405

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1407  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال لْعَّذُّوِّ وَّ إ ِن ْ س ِن ْث َّ  
سَّم َّي ْي َّهُّمْ وَّ ي َّسْي َّع ْق ِزُّ وَّ ي َّزْق َّغُّ ي َّذَّي ْك َّ ق ِى  إَّلْوَّي ْزِ جِي َّالَّ وَّج ْهِك َّ وَّ إ ِن ْ س ِن ْث َّ ق َّي َّحْث َّ  ي َّوْن َّك َّ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Abi Ya’fur that Abu Abdullah {a.s} said:  
"Seek forgiveness from Allah {SWT} during the Witr prayer seventy times, while raising 
your left hand and counting the invocations with your right hand.  
The Messenger of Allah {saws} and his family, used to seek forgiveness from Allah {SWT} in 
the Witr prayer seventy times and would say,  
'This is the position of one seeking refuge in You from the Fire,' seven times."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 489 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 759 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1408  -  وَّ كَّان َّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ سَّي ِّذُّ إَّلْعَّان ِذِي ن ي َّق ُّول 
إَّلس َّحَّزِ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan that Abu Abdullah Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
said: "You may supplicate against the enemy during the Witr prayer, and if you wish, 
you may name them specifically. You should seek forgiveness, raising your hands 
during the Witr prayer level with your face, or, if you wish, you may open your cloak 
(covering) while doing so”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 489 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 131 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 758 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1409  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مَّعْزُّوف ُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّز َّن ُّوذ َّ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا ي َّعْن ِى  إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ و إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
إَّلْوَّي ْزِ: لأَّ إ ِلَّهَّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلل َّهُّ إَّلْحَّلِي مُّ إَّلْكَّزِي مُّ لأَّ إ ِلَّهَّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلل َّهُّ إَّلْعَّلِى  ُّ إَّلْعَّط ِي مُّ سُّن ْحَّان َّ إَّ لل َّهِ رَّب ِّ إَّلس َّمَّاوَّإب ِ إَّلس َّن ْغِ وَّ رَّب ِّ إَّلْأ َّرَّص ِي ن َّ 
إَّلس َّن ْغِ وَّ مَّا ق ِي هِن  َّ وَّ مَّا ن َّي ْن َّهُّن  َّ وَّ رَّب ِّ إَّلْعَّزْس ِ إَّلْعَّط ِي مِ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ َّن ْث ب ِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ   وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ ر َّي ْن ُّ 
إَّلس َّمَّاوَّإب ِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ ج َّمَّالُّ إَّلس َّمَّاوَّإب ِ وَّ  إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عِمَّاذُّ إَّلس َّمَّاوَّإب ِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ ق ِوَّإمُّ 
إَّلس َّمَّاوَّإب ِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ صَّزِي ح ُّ إَّلْمُّسْي َّصْزِج ِي ن َّ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ ع ِي َّاب ُّ إَّلْمُّسْي َّع ِي ي ِي ن َّ وَّ  إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ إَّلْمُّق َّزِّح ُّ عَّن ِ 
إَّ لْمَّكْزُّون ِي ن َّ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ إَّلْمُّزَّوِّحُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّع ْمُّومِي ن َّ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ مُّح ِي ث ُّ ذَّعْوَّة ِ إَّلْمُّص ْطَّزِّي ن َّ وَّ  إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ إ ِلَّهُّ إَّلْعَّالَّمِي ن َّ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ  
إَّلل َّهُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ُّ إَّلز َّجِي مُّ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ كَّاس ِف ُّ إَّلس ُّوءِ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ إَّ لل َّهُّ ن ِك َّ مُّن ْز َّل  كُّل ُّ جَّاج َّه ٍ ي َّا إَّلل َّهُّ لَّي ْسَّ ي َّزُّذ ُّ ع َّص َّن َّك َّ إ ِلأ َّ جِلْمُّك َّ  
وَّ لأَّ ي ُّن ْح ِى  مِن ْ عَّذ َّإن ِك َّ إ ِلأ َّ رَّجْمَّي ُّك َّ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّن ْح ِى  مِن ْك َّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلي  َّص َّز ُّعُّ إ ِلَّي ْك َّ ق َّهَّث ْ لِى  مِن ْ لَّذُّن ْك َّ  ي َّا إ ِلَّهِى  رَّجْمَّه ً ي ُّع ْن ِي ن ِى  ن ِهَّا  
عَّن ْ   رَّجْمَّه ِ مَّن ْ سِوَّإك ٍ ن ِالْق ُّذْرَّة ِ إَّل َّي ِى  ن ِهَّا إ َّجْي َّي ْث َّ ج َّمِي غَّ مَّا ق ِى  إَّلْن ِلاَّذِ وَّ ن ِهَّا ي َّن ْس ُّزُّ مَّي ْث َّ إَّلْعِن َّاذِ  وَّ لأَّ ي ُّهْلِكْن ِى  ع َّم اً جَّي  َّى  
ي َّع ْق ِزَّ لِى  وَّ ي َّزْجَّمَّن ِى  وَّ ي ُّعَّزِّق َّن ِى  إَّلأِسْي ِح َّان َّه َّ ق ِى  ذُّعَّان ِى  وَّ إُّرْر ُّق ْن ِى  إَّلْعَّ اق ِي َّه َّ إ ِلَّى مُّن ْي َّهَّى إ َّج َّلِى  وَّ إ َّق ِلْن ِى  عَّي ْزَّي ِى  وَّ لأَّ 
ي ُّس ْمِث ْ ن ِى  عَّذُّوِّى  وَّ لأَّ ي ُّمَّكِّن ْهُّ مِن ْ رَّق َّن َّي ِى  إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ ِن ْ رَّق َّعْي َّن ِى  ق َّمَّن ْ ذ َّإ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي َّص َّعُّن ِى  وَّ إ ِن ْ وَّ ص َّعْي َّن ِى  ق َّمَّن ْ ذ َّإ إَّل َّذ ِى  
ي َّزْق َّعُّن ِى  وَّ إ ِن ْ إ َّهْلَّكْي َّن ِى  ق َّمَّن ْ  ذ َّإ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي َّحُّولُّ ن َّي ْن َّك َّ وَّ ن َّي ْن ِى  إ َّوْ ي َّي َّعَّز َّض ُّ لَّك َّ ق ِى  س َّى ْءٍ مِن ْ إ َّمْزِى  وَّ ق َّذْ عَّلِمْث ُّ إ َّن ْ لَّي ْسَّ  
ق ِى  جُّكْمِك َّ ط ُّلْم  وَّ لأَّ ق ِى  ن َّق ِمَّي ِك َّ عَّح َّلَّه   إ ِن  َّمَّا ي َّعْح َّلُّ مَّن ْ ي َّح َّاف ُّ إَّلْق َّوْب َّ وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ي َّحْي َّاح ُّ إ ِلَّى إَّلط  ُّ لْمِ إَّلص  َّ عِي ف ُّ وَّ ق َّذْ  
ي َّعَّالَّي ْث َّ عَّن ْ ذ َّلِك َّ ي َّا إ ِلَّهِى  ق َّلاَّ ي َّح ْعَّلْن ِى  لِلْن َّلاَّءِ ع َّزَّص اً وَّ لأَّ لِن َّق ِمَّي ِك َّ ن َّصَّن اً وَّ مَّهِّلْن ِى  وَّ ن َّق ِّسْ ن ِى  وَّ إ َّق ِلْن ِى  عَّي ْزَّي ِى  وَّ لأَّ 
ي ُّي ْن ِعْن ِى  ن ِن َّلاَّءٍ عَّلَّى إ َّي َّزِ ن َّلاَّءٍ ق َّق َّذْ ي َّزَّى ص َّعْق ِى  وَّ ق ِل َّه َّ جِي لَّي ِ ى  إ َّسْي َّعِي ذ ُّ ن ِك َّ إَّلل َّي ْلَّه َّ ق َّا َّعِذ ْن ِى  وَّ إ َّسْي َّح ِي زُّ ن ِك َّ مِن َّ إَّلن  َّارِ 
ق َّا َّج ِزْن ِى  وَّ إ َّسْا َّلُّك َّ إَّلْح َّن  َّه َّ ق َّلاَّ ي َّحْزِمْن ِى  ي ُّم َّ إُّذْعُّ إَّلل َّهَّ ن ِمَّا إ َّجْن َّن ْث َّ وَّ إِسْي َّع ْق ِزِ إَّلل َّهَّ سَّن ْعِ ي ن َّ مَّز َّة ً .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated that Imam Ali ibn al-Hussain {a.s}, the Master of Worshippers (Zain 
al-Abidin), used to say, "Al-‘Afw, Al-‘Afw (Pardon, Pardon)" three hundred times during 
the Witr prayer at the time of Suhoor (the pre-dawn hours). 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 489 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 759 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1410 - وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ إَّلي  ُّمَّالِى ِّ ق َّالَّ: كَّان َّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّولُّ ق ِى  إ ج ِزِ وَّي ْزِةِ وَّ هُّوَّ ق َّان ِم  
«ي َّذَّي ْهِ ج َّمِي عاً ق ُّذ َّإم 
وَّج ْهِهِ وَّ ي َّق ُّول ن َّي ِى  ج َّاص ِعَّه   لَّك َّ لِمَّا إ َّي َّث ْ 
إ َّن َّا ذ َّإ ن َّي ْن َّ ي َّذَّي ْك َّ ق َّح ُّذ ْ لِن َّق ْسِك َّ إَّلزِّص َّا مِن ْ ن َّق ْسِى  جَّي  َّى ي َّزْص َّى لَّك َّ إَّلْعُّي ْن َّى لأَّ إ َّعُّوذُّ لأَّ إ َّعُّوذُّ لأَّ  إ َّعُّوذ الَّ وَّ كَّان َّ وَّ 
إَّلل َّهِ إ ِذ َّإ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Ma'ruf ibn Kharbudh from one of them, meaning Imam 
Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} and Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}:  
"Recite in the Qunoot of Witr:  
'There is no god but Allah {SWT}, the Forbearing, the Generous. There is no god but Allah 
{SWT}, the Most High, the Mighty. Glory be to Allah {SWT}, the Lord {AZJ} of the seven heavens 
and the seven earths, and whatever is within them and between them, and Lord {AZJ} of 
the Great Throne. O’ Allah {SWT}, You are the Light of the heavens and the earth. You are 
Allah {SWT}, the Ornament of the heavens and the earth. You are Allah {SWT}, the Beauty of 
the heavens and the earth. You are Allah {SWT}, the Pillar o f the heavens and the earth. 
You are Allah {SWT}, the Sustainer of the heavens and the earth. You are Allah {SWT}, the 
Helper of those who cry for help. You are Allah {SWT}, the Reliever of the distressed. You 
are Allah {SWT}, the One who answers the prayer of the distressed. You are Allah {SWT}, the 
God of the worlds. You are Allah {SWT}, the Most Merciful, the Most Compassionate. You 
are Allah {SWT}, the Remover of hardship. You are Allah {SWT}; with You every need is 
resolved.  
O’ Allah {SWT}, nothing repels Your anger except Your forbearance. Nothing delivers from 
Your punishment except Your mercy, and nothing can rescue me from You except 
pleading with You. So grant me, O’ my God, mercy from You that suffices me from the 
mercy of any other t han You, by the power which You revived all in the lands and by 
which You revive the dead among Your servants.  
Do not destroy me with anguish until You forgive me, have mercy on me, and make 
known to me the response to my supplication. Grant me well-being until the end of my 
life.  
Overlook my missteps, do not let my enemies gloat over me, and do not grant them 
control over me.  
O’ Allah {SWT}, if You elevate me, who can bring me down? If You bring me down, who 
can elevate me? If You destroy me, who can intervene between You and me or interfere 
in any matter of mine? I know that there is no injustice in Your rule and no haste in Your 
punishment. The one who rushes is he who fears loss, and only the weak resort to 
injustice. You are far above all of this, my God. So do not make me a target for 
affliction, nor a subject for Your wrath.  
Grant me respite, relieve me, forgive my faults, and do not follow one trial with 
another. You see my weakness and lack of resources. I seek refuge with You this night, 
so protect me. I seek Your protection from the Fire, so protect me. I ask You for 
Paradise, so do not deny it to me.'  
Then call upon Allah {SWT} with whatever you wish and seek His forgiveness seventy 
times." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 490 • Meftah al -Falah, Part 1, p. 326 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, 
p. 759 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 84, p. 203 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 84, p. 268 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1411  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال   إَّلْوَّي ْزِ إِسْي ِع ْق َّار  وَّ ق ِى   
إَّلْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ إَّلذ ُّعَّاءُّ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Abu Hamzah al -Thumali that Imam Ali ibn al -Hussain {a.s} 
would say in the last part of his Witr prayer while standing:  
"My Lord {AZJ}, I have committed wrongs and wronged myself, and what a terrible thing 
I have done. Here are my hands as recompense for what they have done."  
Then he would raise both his hands before his face and say:  
"And here is my neck, humbled before You for what it has done."  
He would then bow his head and humble his neck and continue:  
"Here I am before You; take from my soul the satisfaction that pleases You until You 
are content. To You belongs all repentance. I will not return, I will not return, I will not 
return [to sin]."  
It is reported that by Allah {SWT}, when He said: "I will not return," he never returned [to 
the sin].  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 491 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 762 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 84, 
p. 276 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 4, p. 416 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 71, H. 1410

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1412  - وَّ كَّان َّ إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّذْعُّو ق ِى  ق ُّن ُّوب ِ إَّلْوَّي ْزِ ن ِهَّذ َّإ إَّلذ ُّعَّاءِ ي َّن ِى  ن ِي َّق ْذِي زٍ وَّ ي َّذْن ِي زٍ وَّ 
ي َّن ْصِي زٍ ن ِع َّي ْزِ ي َّق ْصِي زٍ وَّ إ َّج ْزَّج ْي َّن ِى  مِن ْ   ن ِحَّوْلِك َّ وَّ  ق ُّو َّي ِك َّ إ ُّجَّاوِلُّ إَّلذ ُّن ْي َّا ي ُّم َّ إ ُّر َّإوِلُّهَّا ي ُّم َّ إ ُّر َّإي ِلُّهَّا وَّ إ ي َّي ْي َّن ِى   
ق ِي هَّا إَّلْكَّلَّا َّ وَّ إَّلْمَّزْعَّى وَّ ن َّص َّزْي َّن ِى  ق ِي هَّا إَّلْهُّذَّى ق َّن ِعْمَّ إَّلز َّب  ُّ إ َّن ْث َّ وَّ ن ِعْمَّ إَّلْمَّوْلَّى ق َّي َّا مَّن ْ  كَّز َّمَّن ِى  وَّ س َّز َّق َّن ِى  وَّ ن َّع َّمَّن ِى  إ َّعُّوذ ُّ  
ن ِك َّ مِن َّ إَّلز  َّق  ُّومِ وَّ إ َّعُّوذ ُّ ن ِك َّ مِن َّ إَّلْحَّمِي مِ وَّ إ َّعُّوذ ُّ ن ِك َّ مِن ْ مَّق ِي لٍ ق ِى  إَّلن  َّارِ ن َّي ْن َّ إ َّطْن َّاق ِ إَّلن  َّارِ   ق ِى  ط ِلاَّلِ إَّلن  َّارِ ي َّوْمَّ إَّلن  َّارِ ي َّا  
رَّب  َّ إَّلن  َّارِ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ ِن ِّى  إ َّسْا َّلُّك َّ مَّق ِي لاً ق ِى  إَّلْح َّن  َّه ِ ن َّي ْن َّ إ َّن ْهَّارِ هَّا وَّ إ َّس ْح َّارِهَّا وَّ ي ِمَّارِهَّا وَّ رَّي ْحَّان ِهَّا وَّ ج َّذَّمِهَّا وَّ إ َّر ْوَّإج ِهَّا إَّلل َّهُّم َّ 
إ ِن ِّى  إ َّسْا َّلُّك َّ ج َّي ْزَّ إَّلْح َّي ْزِ رِص ْوَّإن َّك َّ وَّ إَّلْح َّن  َّه َّ وَّ إ َّعُّوذ ُّ ن ِك َّ مِن ْ س َّزِّ إَّلس  َّزِّ سَّح َّطِك َّ وَّ إَّلن  َّارِ هَّذ َّ إ مَّق َّامُّ إَّلْعَّان ِذ ِ ن ِك َّ مِن َّ إَّلن  َّا رِ  
ي َّلاَّب َّ مَّز َّإب ٍ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إِج ْعَّلْ ج َّوْق َّك َّ ق ِى  ج َّسَّذِى  كُّلِّهِ وَّ إِج ْعَّلْ ق َّلْن ِى  إ َّس َّذ َّ مَّح َّاق َّه ً لَّك َّ مِم َّا هُّوَّ وَّ إِج ْعَّلْ لِى  ق ِى  كُّلِّ ي َّوْمٍ وَّ 
لَّي ْلَّه ٍ جَّط  اً وَّ ن َّصِي ن اً مِن ْ عَّمَّلٍ ن ِطَّاعَّي ِك َّ وَّ إِي ِّن َّاعِ مَّزْص َّاي ِك َّ إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ َّن ْث َّ  مُّن ْي َّهَّى ع َّاي َّي ِى  وَّ رَّج َّان ِى  وَّ مَّسْا َّلَّي ِى  وَّ طَّلِن َّي ِى  
إ َّسْا َّلُّك َّ ي َّا إ ِلَّهِى  كَّمَّالَّ إَّلْأ ِي مَّان ِ وَّ ي َّمَّامَّ إَّلْي َّق ِي ن ِ وَّ صِذْق َّ إَّلي  َّوَّك ُّلِ عَّلَّي ْك َّ وَّ جُّسْن َّ إَّلط  َّن ِّ ن ِك َّ  ي َّا سَّي ِّذِى  إِج ْعَّلْ إ ِجْسَّان ِى  
مُّص َّاعَّق اً وَّ صَّلاَّي ِى  ي َّص َّز ُّعاً وَّ  ذُّعَّان ِى  مُّسْي َّح َّان اً وَّ عَّمَّلِى  مَّق ْن ُّولأً وَّ سَّعْي ِى  مَّس ْكُّورإً وَّ ذ َّن ْن ِى  مَّع ْق ُّورإً وَّ لَّق ِّن ِى  مِن ْك َّ  
« وَّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّى مُّحَّم َّذٍ وَّ إ لِهِ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Abdur-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah from Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} that 
He said:  
"The Qunoot (supplication) in the Witr prayer is for seeking forgiveness, while in the 
obligatory (prayers) it is for supplication (general prayers)”.  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 340 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 450 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 
491 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 131 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 755 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 276 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar: 1413  -  وروى محمذ ن ن  مسلم عن  إ ن ى  ج عق ز علي ه إلسلام ق ال: " إلق ن وب  ق ى  كل ركعي ي ن  ق ى  إلي طوع وإلق زي ص ه  .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} would supplicate in 
the Qunoot of Witr with this supplication: 
"O’ Allah {SWT}, You created me with precise measurement, arrangement, and foresight 
without any negligence. You brought me out of 'three layers of darkness' by Your 
strength and power. I attempt to engage with this world, then I turn away from it, and 
then I leave it be hind. You provided me with sustenance and care in it and guided me 
within it, so blessed is the Lord {AZJ} and excellent is the Master. O’ the One who honored 
and elevated me and bestowed upon me Your bounties, I seek refuge in You from the 
tree of Zaqqum, I seek refuge in You from boiling water (al-Hameem), and I seek refuge 
in You from a resting place in the Fire, amidst the layers of the Fire, under the shadows 
of the Fire on the Day of Fire. O’ Lord {AZJ} of the Fire! O’ Allah {SWT}, I ask You for a place 
to rest in Paradise among its rivers, trees, fruits, sweet scents, attendants, and 
spouses. O’ Allah {SWT}, I ask You for the best of all good - Your pleasure and Paradise, 
and I seek refuge in You from the worst of all evil - Your wrath and the Fire. This is the 
station of one seeking refuge in You from the Fire." (He would repeat this three times). 
"O’ Allah {SWT}, place Your fear throughout my body and make my heart fear You more 
intensely than it does now. Grant me, in every day and night, a share and portion of 
deeds dedicated to Your obedience and seeking Your pleasure. O’ Allah {SWT}, You are 
the ultimate aim of my efforts, my hope, my requests, and my needs. I ask You, O’ my 
God, for perfect faith, complete certainty, sincere relian ce upon You, and a good 
opinion of You, my Master. Multiply my good deeds, make my prayers an expression 
of humility, accept my supplications, approve of my actions, thank my efforts, forgive 
my sins, and grant me 'radiance and joy' from You. May Allah {SWT} bless Muhammad 
and his family." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V.1, p. 491 • Al-Wafi, V. 8, p. 761 • Bihar Al-Anwar, V. 84, p. 269 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar: 1414  - وروى عن ه ر رإرة  إ ن ه ق ال: " إلق ن وب  ق ى  كل إلصلوإب .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} 
who said:  
"There is Qunoot (a supplication) in every two rak'ahs of the optional (supererogatory) 
and obligatory prayers." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1415   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن َّان ُّ ن ْن ُّ عُّي ْمَّان َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى :  إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ُّسَّمِّى  إَّلْأ َّن ِم َّه َّ عَّلَّي هِمُّ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق ِى  
إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Him (Imam Abu Ja'far {a.s}) that He said:  
"Qunoot (a supplication) is to be recited in all prayers." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar: 1416  - وق ال علي ه إلسلام: " كل ما ن اج ي ث  ن ه رن ك  ق ى  إلصلاة  ق لي س ن كلام .
hadiths.en:
Aban ibn Uthman narrated from Al -Halabi that he said to Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}: "Shall I mention the names of the Imams {a.s} during the 
prayer?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Summarize them”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 493 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 131 • Tahdhib Al -
Ahkam, Part 2, p. 326 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 886 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1417   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  وَّلأ َّذٍ جَّق ْضِ ن ْن ِ سَّالِمٍ إَّلْحَّن  َّاطِ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول 
ن ِا َّن ْ ي ُّصَّلِّى َّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ مِن َّ إَّلْوَّي ْزِ ي ُّم َّ ي َّن ْصَّزِف َّ ق َّي َّق ْص ِى َّ جَّاج َّي َّهُّ ي ُّم َّ ي َّزْج ِغَّ ق َّي ُّصَّلِّى َّ رَّكْ عَّه ً .  
ولأ ن ا س إ ن  ي صلى  إلزج ل ركعي ي ن  من  إلوي ز ي م ي س زب  إلماء وي ي كلم وي ن كح  وي ق ص ى  ما س اء من  جاج ه  وي حذب  وص وءإ ي م  
ي صلى  إلزكعه  ق ن ل إ ن  ي صلى  إلع ذإة  .
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said:  
"Whatever you whisper to your Lord {AZJ} in prayer is not considered speech”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1418   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  عَّن ِ إَّلْق ُّن ُّوب ِ ق ِى  إَّلْوَّي ْزِ ق َّال 
ن َّسِي ث ُّ إ َّق ْن ُّث ُّ إ ِذ َّإ رَّق َّعْث ُّ رَّإ ْسِى  ق َّق َّال . 
ق ال مصن ف  هذ إ إلكي اب : جكم من  ي ن سى إلق ن وب  جي ى ي زكغ إ ن  ي ق ن ث  إ ذ إ رق غ رإ سه من  إلزكوع، وإ ن ما من غ إلصاذق  علي ه  
إلسلام من   ذ لك  ق ى  إلوي ز وإلع ذإة  ج لاق ا للعامه  لأ ن هم ي ق ن ي ون  ق ي هما ن عذ إلزكوع وإ ن ما إ طلق  ذ لك  ق ى  سان ز إلصلوإب  لأن   
ج مهور إلعامه  لأ ي زون  إلق ن وب  ق ي ها ق ا ذ إ ق زع  إلأن سان  من  إلوي ز صلى ركعي ى  إلق ح ز
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abu Walad Hafs bin Salim Al -Hannat that He said:  
"I heard Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} say,  
'There is no harm if a person prays two units (rak‘ahs) of Witr prayer, then turns away 
to fulfil a need, and then returns to pray one unit (rak‘ah).'".  
[AL SADUQ] 
There is no issue if a person prays two rak‘ahs of Witr, then drinks water, speaks, 
engages in intimacy, fulfils any need, or renews ablution, and then performs the 
remaining single rak‘ah before the Fajr prayer. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 493 • Meftah al-Falah, Part 1, p. 323 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1419   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
« وَّ ق ِى  إَّلي  َّان ِي َّه ِ إَّلْحَّمْذَّ و .  
وي ح ور  للزج ل إ ن  ي حس وهم ق ى  صلاة  إللي ل جس وإ   وكلما ق زب  من  إلق ح ز ق هو إ ق ص ل ق ا ذ إ طلغ إلق ح ز ق صل إلع ذإة  وإق صل 
ن ي ن  ركعي ى  إلق ح ز ون ي ن  إلع ذإة  ن اص طح اع وي ح ز ي ك  إلي سلي م
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiya bin Ammar asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} bout the 
position of Qunoot (supplication) in the Witr prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: "It is done before the bowing (ruku‘)."  
Mu'awiya then asked: "What if I forget it?"  
Abu Abdullah {a.s} replied: "No (do not perform it after lifting your head from bowing)."  
[AL SADUQ] 
The author of this book stated: If someone forgets the Qunoot until they bow (ruku'), 
they should perform the Qunoot when they raise their head from the bowing position.  
However, Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s}, prohibited this for Witr and Fajr (Dawn) prayers, differing 
from the general practice of the public, as they perform Qunoot after the bowing. He 
permitted this for other prayers because the majority of the public do not see Qunoot 
as necessary in them. When a person completes the Witr prayer, they should proceed 
to pray the two rak'ahs of Fajr. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 71, H. 1418

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Pray the two rak'ahs of Fajr (supererogatory prayer) before Fajr, at its time, or shortly 
thereafter.  
In the first rak'ah, recite Surah Al -Fatiha and ‘Qul ya -ayyuhal kafirun’  (Surah al -
Kafirun),  
In the second rak'ah, recite Al-Hamd and 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas)." 
[AL SADUQ] 
It is permissible for a person to combine (or perform rapidly) multiple units in the night 
prayer, and the closer it is to Fajr (dawn), the more virtuous it is.  
When Fajr begins, perform the Fajr prayer and separate between the two units of Fajr 
and the main Fajr prayer by lying down briefly.  
And the Tasleem (salutation ending the prayer) suffices for you.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 493 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 318 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1421   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ سَّعِي ذٍ إَّلْأ َّعْزَّح ِ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ج ُّعِلْث ُّ ق ِذَّإك َّ إ ِن ى  إ َّكُّون ُّ ق ِى  إَّلْوَّي ْزِ وَّ 
إ َّكُّون ُّ ق َّذْ ن َّوَّي ْث ُّ  إَّلص َّوْمَّ وَّ إ َّكُّون ُّ ق ِى  إَّلذ ُّعَّاءِ وَّ إ َّج َّاف ُّ إَّلْق َّح ْزَّ وَّ إ َّكْزَّةُّ إ َّن ْ إ َّق ْطَّغَّ عَّلَّى ن َّق ْسِى  إَّلذ ُّعَّاءَّ وَّ إ َّس ْزَّب َّ إَّ لْمَّاءَّ وَّ 
ي َّكُّون ُّ إَّلْق ُّل َّه ُّ إ َّمَّامِى  ق َّالَّ ق َّق َّالَّ لِى س ْزَّب ْ وَّ إِرْج ِغْ إ ِلَّى مَّكَّان ِك َّ وَّ لأَّ 
ي َّق ْطَّغْ عَّلَّى ن َّق ْسِك َّ إَّلذ ُّعَّاءَّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"What ending is more complete than the Tasleem (salutation)?"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 71. THE SUPPLICATION OF QUNOOT IN WITR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1422  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ن َّ رَّن ِّى َّ إَّلْمَّلِك ِ  
إَّلْق ُّذ ُّوسِ إَّلْعَّز ِي ز ِ إَّلْحَّكِي مِ ي َّلاَّب َّ مَّز َّإب ٍ ي ُّم َّ ي َّق ُّولُّ ي َّا جَّى  ُّ ي َّا ق َّي  ُّومُّ ي َّا ن َّز ُّ ي َّا رَّجِ ي مُّ ي َّا ع َّن ِى  ُّ ي َّا كَّزِي مُّ إُّرْر ُّق ْن ِى  مِن َّ إَّلي ِّح َّارَّة ِ 
إ َّعْط َّمَّهَّا ق َّص ْلاً وَّ إ َّوْسَّعَّهَّا رِر ْق اً وَّ ج َّي ْزَّهَّا لِى  عَّاق ِن َّه ً ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ لأَّ ج َّي ْزَّ ق ِي مَّا لأَّ عَّاق ِن َّه َّ لَّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Sa'id al-A'raj that He said:  
I said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}  
"May I be your ransom, sometimes I am in the Witr prayer and have already intended 
to fast. I am engaged in supplication and I fear the approach of dawn, but I dislike 
interrupting my supplication to drink water, and there is a water jug in front of me."  
Imam {a.s} said to me: "Take a step, two, or three towards it, drink, and then return to 
your place without interrupting your supplication." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 494 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 7, p. 279 
﴿HADITH ﴾
